title: Print a scoped traffic light enum
prompt_md:
## Goal
Define a scoped `TrafficLight` enum and overload `operator<<` to print its name.

## Input
None (hardcoded values).

## Output
Exact lines:
```
red
yellow
green
```
(trailing newline after `green`)

## Requirements
1. Define `enum class TrafficLight` with values `red`, `yellow`, `green` (in that order).
2. Overload `std::ostream& operator<<(std::ostream& out, TrafficLight t)` using a `switch` statement with fully qualified case labels (`TrafficLight::red`, etc.).
3. Each case prints the lowercase name.
4. The overload must return `out`.
5. `main` prints all three values, each on its own line.

title: Describe a card suit
prompt_md:
## Goal
Complete the function `describeSuit` that takes a scoped `Suit` enum value and returns a `std::string` description.

## Input
None (hardcoded values in `main`).

## Output
Exact lines:
```
Clubs: low suit
Diamonds: low suit
Hearts: high suit
Spades: high suit
```
(trailing newline after `Spades: high suit`)

## Requirements
1. `enum class Suit` is defined with values `clubs`, `diamonds`, `hearts`, `spades` (in that order).
2. Complete `std::string describeSuit(Suit s)` which returns `"low suit"` for `clubs` and `diamonds`, and `"high suit"` for `hearts` and `spades`.
3. Use a `switch` statement with fully qualified case labels inside `describeSuit`.
4. `main` is already written — do not modify it.

## Notes
- Combine `enum class` access with a `std::string` return value.
- The `operator<<` for `Suit` is already provided in the starter.

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Clubs: low suit
Diamonds: low suit
Hearts: high suit
Spades: high suit
test_cases.label: Sample test 1

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Clubs: low suit
Diamonds: low suit
Hearts: high suit
Spades: high suit
test_cases.label: Hidden test 2

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Clubs: low suit
Diamonds: low suit
Hearts: high suit
Spades: high suit
test_cases.label: Hidden test 3
